channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Jannik Sinner leads Italy past the Netherlands for its second consecutive Davis Cup title
item.description: MALAGA, Spain (AP) — Jannik Sinner clinched Italy’s second consecutive Davis Cup title and capped his breakthrough season at the top of tennis by beating Tallon Griekspoor 7-6 (2), 6-2 on Sunday for a 2-0 win over the Netherlands in the final of the team competition.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Salah double sends Liverpool 8 points clear in Premier League as United draws first game with Amorim
item.description: It has been more than 30 years since a team held as big a lead as Liverpool after 12 rounds of the Premier League. Not that Arne Slot is getting too excited about an eight-point advantage before even hitting December.
item.dc:creator: STEVE DOUGLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bears vs. Vikings: What you need to know for Sunday's NFC North showdown at Soldier Field
item.description: CHICAGO — To paraphrase the famous words of then-Indianapolis Colts head coach Jim Mora, the Bears aren't really in a position to think playoffs right now. They're just hoping they can win a game.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Russell and Hamilton give Mercedes a 1-2 finish in Las Vegas
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — An ecstatic George Russell had just won the Formula 1 race Saturday night when he exclaimed in his radio, “Vegas!” He was ready to start celebrating in America's party capital, saying he would skip his flight back to his home in the United Kingdom to make the most of his Las
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arnold, Robinson run for more than 100 yards as Oklahoma stuns No. 7 Alabama 24-3
item.description: Xavier Robinson ran for career highs of 107 yards and two touchdowns, and Oklahoma stunned No. 7 Alabama 24-3 to become bowl eligible and deal a severe blow to the Crimson Tide’s chances of receiving a College Football Playoff berth.
item.dc:creator: Cliff Brunt, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Penguins' Crosby scores 600th NHL goal
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Sidney Crosby would’ve liked his 600th NHL goal to come in a win Crosby became the 21sth player to reach the mark when scored in the second period of the Pittsburgh Penguins' 6-1 loss to the Utah Hockey Club on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: DAN SCIFO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michigan's new No. 1 recruit photographed with subject of sign-stealing scandal before Wolverines' game
item.description: Michigan's new No. 1 recruit, Bryce Underwood, was taking in the sights in Ann Arbor before a game Saturday and posed for a photo with Connor Stalions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida knocks No. 9 Ole Miss out of College Football Playoff contention, 24-17 in the Swamp
item.description: Coach Billy Napier and his team did something no one at Florida had done in nearly two decades. It could be the turning point for a once-proud program that clearly slipped in recent years.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Michkov scores in overtime, Flyers rally to beat Blackhawks 3-2
item.description: Matvei Michkov scored a power-play goal in overtime as the Flyers overcame a two-goal deficit in the third period to beat the Chicago Blackhawks 3-2 on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: No. 11 Miami pulls away late to beat Wake Forest 42-14 and move one win from the ACC title game
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Wake Forest figured it wouldn't keep Miami's offense quiet forever. To their chagrin, the Demon Deacons were right.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ohio State ends Indiana's undefeated campaign convincingly, putting damper on Hoosiers' playoff hopes
item.description: The fifth-ranked Indiana Hoosiers were walloped on the road against No. 2 Ohio State, ending their undefeated season and putting a damper on their playoff hopes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LaVine, Dosunmu and White power the Bulls to a 136-122 win over the Hawks
item.description: Zach LaVine scored 26 points, Ayo Dosunmu and Coby White each had 20 and the Chicago Bulls beat the Atlanta Hawks 136-122 on Friday night in an NBA Cup game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Purdy, Bosa out for Green Bay matchup, Allen steps up as QB
item.description: (KRON) – San Francisco 49ers head coach Kyle Shanahan announced in a press conference Friday that quarterback Brock Purdy will be out for Sunday’s game against the Green Bay Packers. Purdy injured his right shoulder in last Sunday's loss to the Seattle Seahawks.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: No ball at Tropicana Field in 2026 after Milton; new ballpark deal dies: Tampa Bay Rays
item.description: The St. Petersburg City Council has voted to spend over $23 million to repair Tropicana Field after Hurricane Milton destroyed the roof on the home of the Tampa Bay Rays.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, Fox 5

item.title: Giants release quarterback Daniel Jones just days after benching him
item.description: EAST RUTHERFORD, N.J. (AP) — The Daniel Jones era in New York is over. The Giants quarterback was granted his release on Friday by the team just days after the franchise said it was benching him in favor of third-stringer Tommy DeVito.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Jazz blow 20-point lead to Spurs, lose fourth straight
item.description: SAN ANTONIO, Tex. (AP) - Harrison Barnes had 25 points and 10 rebounds, rookie Stephon Castle scored 18 points and the San Antonio Spurs rallied to defeat the Utah Jazz 126-118 on Thursday night for their second straight win without Victor Wembanyama.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: CMU women's soccer "feeling pretty good" ahead of NCAA tournament
item.description: Colorado Mesa women’s soccer is in. The Mavs received an at-large bid into the Division II NCAA tournament.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: LeBron James announces he's leaving social media
item.description: LeBron James is taking a break from social media, citing negativity online. The Lakers star posted a screengrab of a post from Rich Kleiman, Kevin Durant's longtime agent, to X, formerly Twitter.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Alex Killorn helps the Ducks beat the Blackhawks 3-2 for their third straight win
item.description: Alex Killorn had a goal and an assist in the third period, and the Anaheim Ducks beat the Chicago Blackhawks 3-2 on Tuesday night for their season-high third straight win.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Jets are a dumpster fire
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The Jets are a remarkable dumpster fire. Head coach Robert Saleh fired. GM Joe Douglas fired. Offensive coordinator Nathaniel Hackett demoted. They are a 3 and 8 football team and will most certainly miss the playoffs for the 14th consecutive year.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Connor Bedard trying to break out of slump as the Blackhawks struggle to score
item.description: Bedard was terrific last season, living up to the considerable hype that has followed him around for years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bulls great Bob Love dead at 81 after battle with cancer
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — An all-star for the Chicago Bulls in the 1970's, franchise great Bob Love has died at the age of 81 after a long battle with cancer, the team announced Monday. Love played from 1968 to 1976 with the Chicago Bulls and was affectionately nicknamed "Butterbean" for his kind demeanor.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Another Lakers legend to receive statue outside arena
item.description: One of the winningest coaches in NBA history will forever be enshrined in downtown Los Angeles when the Lakers unveil the newest statue on Star Plaza outside Crypto.com Arena.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump dance takes over sports world
item.description: Supporters of President-elect Donald Trump have been paying homage on the gridiron and inside the octagon in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Southwest employee welcomes Chiefs fans by playing Bills song over intercom
item.description: She said she wanted to let Chiefs fans know that "the Buffalo Bills have the best fans in the league.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Minkewicz, NewsNation

item.title: Report: Kris Byrant's Lamborghini Huracan stolen from Colorado home
item.description: Kris Bryant's Lamborghini Huracan was stolen by a multi-state vehicle theft ring, but was recovered by police in Las Vegas after they traced the stolen vehicle to a home in the city.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9
